title: oreoselis pisana
intro: Oreoselis pisana, also known as Sweetroot or American Sweetroot, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible roots. The roots of Oreoselis pisana have been used in traditional medicine for their sweet, earthy flavor and numerous medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Oreoselis pisana include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce swelling. Herbal preparations of Oreoselis pisana typically involve the root being dried and then used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
